narrative_thread: Player has removed their pajamas, leaving them on the bed. They are now wearing only their underwear.
blueprint: The Kitchen is now fully realized, connecting to the Hallway (north), Pantry (west), and Dining Nook (south). The Pantry and Dining Nook remain planned for further expansion.

player.description: You are standing in your bedroom, wearing nothing but a pair of comfortable cotton underwear. The elastic is starting to wear thin, but they’ll do for now. Your skin prickles slightly in the cool morning air, and you resist the urge to hug yourself for warmth. Pussycat’s gaze is particularly unnerving at this moment.

room_start.name: Your Bedroom
room_start.description:
The bedroom is a cozy, if slightly cluttered, space that smells faintly of lavender and last night’s takeout. Your queen-sized bed, still rumpled from sleep, sits against the west wall, its sheets tangled in a way that suggests a restless night. To the east, a partially open door leads to the hallway, where the faint sound of a dripping faucet echoes. A dresser stands against the north wall, its surface littered with an assortment of lotions, hair ties, and a half-empty glass of water. The morning light spills through the blinds on the south wall, casting long shadows across the hardwood floor. Pussycat, your calico cat, is curled up on a pile of laundry near the foot of the bed, watching you with an unblinking stare that seems to say, "You’re late."

You can see here: bed, dresser, pile of laundry, alarm clock, half-empty glass of water, Pussycat, bedroom door, window blinds, pajamas.
room_start.base_description: The bedroom is a cozy, if slightly cluttered, space that smells faintly of lavender and last night’s takeout. Your queen-sized bed, still rumpled from sleep, sits against the west wall, its sheets tangled in a way that suggests a restless night. To the east, a partially open door leads to the hallway, where the faint sound of a dripping faucet echoes. A dresser stands against the north wall, its surface littered with an assortment of lotions, hair ties, and a half-empty glass of water. The morning light spills through the blinds on the south wall, casting long shadows across the hardwood floor. Pussycat, your calico cat, is curled up on a pile of laundry near the foot of the bed, watching you with an unblinking stare that seems to say, "You’re late."

room_565c7328.name: The Hallway
room_565c7328.description:
The hallway stretches before you, a narrow but well-trodden path connecting the various domains of your suburban sanctuary. The walls, painted a soothing eggshell white, are adorned with a smattering of framed photos—some of you grinning at the camera, others of Pussycat in various states of feline indignance. The hardwood floor, cool beneath your bare feet, creaks softly with each step, as if whispering secrets about the night’s restless tossing and turning. To the west, your bedroom door stands ajar, its familiar clutter just visible beyond. To the north, a slightly ajar door reveals the tiled expanse of the bathroom, where the persistent *drip... drip...* of a faucet echoes like a metronome counting down to your inevitable tardiness. Southward, the hallway opens into the kitchen, where the faint scent of stale coffee lingers, mingling with the buttery aroma of something you *might* have time to toast. To the east, the hallway widens into the living room, where the morning light spills through the front window, illuminating a trail of Pussycat’s fur that leads toward her favorite napping spot on the couch.

You can see here: framed photos, hallway rug, light switch, sticky note.

Southward, the hallway opens into the kitchen, where the faint scent of stale coffee lingers, mingling with the buttery aroma of something you *might* have time to toast. To the east, the hallway widens into the living room, where the morning light spills through the front window, illuminating a trail of Pussycat’s fur that leads toward her favorite napping spot on the couch.

room_8c6bf631.name: The Bathroom
room_8c6bf631.description:
The bathroom is a cramped but functional space, its walls lined with pale blue tiles that catch the morning light filtering through the frosted window above the shower. The air is thick with the scent of damp towels and the faint, lingering aroma of last night’s lavender-scented soap. A single fluorescent bulb hums overhead, casting a sterile glow over the porcelain sink, its surface marred by a few stray droplets and a smudge of toothpaste. The mirror above it is slightly fogged, as if reluctant to reveal the full extent of your sleep-deprived visage. To the south, the hallway beckons, while a narrow door to the west—slightly ajar—leads to what might be a linen closet or perhaps a forgotten corner of domestic chaos.

You can see here: toothbrush, damp towel, leaky faucet, medicine cabinet, linen closet door, folded towels, fabric softener bottle, lumpy laundry pile.

The air is thick with the scent of damp towels and the faint, lingering aroma of last night’s lavender-scented soap. A single fluorescent bulb hums overhead, casting a sterile glow over the porcelain sink, its surface marred by a few stray droplets and a smudge of toothpaste. The mirror above it is slightly fogged, as if reluctant to reveal the full extent of your sleep-deprived visage. To the south, the hallway beckons, while a narrow door to the west—slightly ajar—leads to what might be a linen closet or perhaps a forgotten corner of domestic chaos.

items.name: toothpaste tube
items.description: A tube of toothpaste, squeezed haphazardly from the middle. The cap is missing, and the paste inside is starting to dry out. It smells faintly of mint and regret.

items.name: ibuprofen bottle
items.description: A small, plastic bottle of ibuprofen. The label is faded, and the expiration date is long past. The pills inside rattle when shaken, but you’re not sure if they’re still safe to take. Probably not worth the risk.

items.name: Band-Aid
items.description: A single, sterile Band-Aid in its wrapper. The edges are slightly yellowed with age, and the adhesive might not hold, but it’s better than nothing.

items.name: razor blade
items.description: A rusted razor blade, its edges still sharp despite the corrosion. It’s seen better days, but it could still draw blood if handled carelessly.

items.name: unmarked vial
items.description: A small, glass vial filled with unmarked pills. The label is missing, and the contents rattle ominously when shaken. You have no idea what they are, and you’re not sure you want to know.

items.name: newspaper clipping
items.description: A yellowed newspaper clipping about a local missing person. The photo is grainy, but the face is eerily familiar. The article is dated years ago, and the details are vague. You don’t remember much about the case, but something about it sends a chill down your spine.

room_37c43cda.name: The Kitchen
room_37c43cda.description:
The kitchen greets you with the warm, slightly stale embrace of a room that has seen countless hurried mornings and late-night snacks. The linoleum floor, a checkerboard of faded yellow and white, is cool underfoot, though a few crumbs cling stubbornly to the grout lines like tiny, edible barnacles. Sunlight streams through the window over the sink, casting a golden rectangle onto the countertop, where a half-empty bag of bread and a toaster with a single, forgotten crumb sit in quiet companionship. The air is thick with the scent of yesterday’s coffee—bitter and lingering—and the faint, buttery promise of something you *might* have time to toast if you hurry. To the north, the hallway beckons, its framed photos and creaking floorboards leading back toward the bathroom and your bedroom. To the west, a door stands slightly ajar, revealing the dim interior of the pantry, while to the south, the kitchen opens into a small dining nook, where a table cluttered with mail and a single, wilting houseplant awaits your attention. The refrigerator hums softly, a steady drone that underscores the quiet chaos of the room.

You can see here: half-empty bag of bread, toaster, refrigerator, coffee maker.

The linoleum floor, a checkerboard of faded yellow and white, is cool underfoot, though a few crumbs cling stubbornly to the grout lines like tiny, edible barnacles. Sunlight streams through the window over the sink, casting a golden rectangle onto the countertop, where a half-empty bag of bread and a toaster with a single, forgotten crumb sit in quiet companionship. The air is thick with the scent of yesterday’s coffee—bitter and lingering—and the faint, buttery promise of something you *might* have time to toast if you hurry. To the north, the hallway beckons, its framed photos and creaking floorboards leading back toward the bathroom and your bedroom. To the west, a door stands slightly ajar, revealing the dim interior of the pantry, while to the south, the kitchen opens into a small dining nook, where a table cluttered with mail and a single, wilting houseplant awaits your attention. The refrigerator hums softly, a steady drone that underscores the quiet chaos of the room.

bed.name: bed
bed.description: A well-loved queen-sized bed with a slightly lumpy mattress. The sheets are a soft cotton blend, rumpled from a night of tossing and turning. The blanket, a gift from your grandmother, is draped haphazardly over the foot of the bed. Despite its disarray, it’s undeniably comfortable—though getting out of it is another matter entirely.

dresser.name: dresser
dresser.description: A sturdy oak dresser with five drawers, each slightly ajar. The top is a chaotic landscape of beauty products, loose change, and a framed photo of you and your college friends at a music festival. The drawers contain your clothes, though you’re not entirely sure which drawer holds what at the moment. One of them creaks ominously when opened.

laundry_pile.name: pile of laundry
laundry_pile.description: A precarious mountain of freshly washed clothes, folded but not yet put away. Mostly work blouses, a few pairs of slacks, and—oh no—your favorite black cardigan is buried somewhere in there. Pussycat has claimed this as her throne, and disturbing her would be unwise. The pile smells faintly of fabric softener and regret.

alarm_clock.name: alarm clock
alarm_clock.description: A small, obnoxious digital alarm clock with glowing red numbers. It’s currently displaying 7:15 AM in bold, accusatory digits. The snooze button is worn smooth from overuse, and the alarm itself is a shrill beep that could wake the dead—or at least your neighbors. You’ve been meaning to replace it for months.

glass_of_water.name: half-empty glass of water
glass_of_water.description: A tall glass of water, now lukewarm and slightly stale. You remember filling it last night before bed, but you must have forgotten to drink it. The glass is smudged with fingerprints, and there’s a faint ring of lipstick on the rim. Hydration is important, but so is not drinking day-old water.

pussycat.name: Pussycat
pussycat.description: A plump, self-satisfied calico cat with a coat that looks like it was painted by a tipsy artist. Her green eyes gleam with mischief, and her tail flicks lazily as she watches you. Pussycat is equal parts affectionate and tyrannical, and she has a habit of sitting on whatever you need most at any given moment. Right now, that appears to be your clean laundry.

bedroom_door.name: bedroom door
bedroom_door.description: A standard interior door, painted white with a brass knob that’s seen better days. It’s currently ajar, leading to the hallway beyond. The door creaks slightly when opened, a sound that always makes you wince—especially when you’re trying to sneak out of the house early.

blinds.name: window blinds
blinds.description: A set of white venetian blinds covering the window on the south wall. They’re slightly dusty, and one of the slats is bent at an awkward angle, letting in a sliver of sunlight that’s currently spotlighting Pussycat’s tail. The cord to adjust them is tangled, as usual.

pajamas.name: pajamas
pajamas.description: A set of soft, slightly worn pajamas in a faded floral pattern. The top is a short-sleeved button-up, and the bottoms are loose, drawstring pants. They’re comfortable, but not exactly office-appropriate. The fabric smells faintly of fabric softener and the faintest hint of last night’s microwave popcorn.

phone.name: smartphone
phone.description: A sleek smartphone with a cracked screen, held together by a case that says "World’s Okayest Employee." The battery is at 42%, which is either a good sign or a ticking time bomb, depending on how your morning goes. There’s a notification from your boss: "Don’t forget the meeting at 9!" Too late for that.

underwear.name: underwear
underwear.description: A pair of comfortable, if unremarkable, cotton underwear. They’re clean, which is the most important thing at the moment. The elastic is starting to wear thin, but they’ll do for another day.

item_hallway_photos.name: framed photos
item_hallway_photos.description: A haphazard collection of framed photos lines the hallway walls. Some are recent—you at a friend’s wedding, Pussycat mid-sneeze—while others are relics from your childhood, their colors faded with time. One photo, slightly crooked, shows you and an ex at a beach, both of you sunburned and grinning. You’re not sure why you still keep it up. Maybe it’s the frame.

item_hallway_rug.name: hallway rug
item_hallway_rug.description: A thin, threadbare runner rug stretches down the length of the hallway, its once-vibrant geometric pattern now muted by years of foot traffic. Pussycat has claimed it as her personal racetrack, and the edges are slightly frayed from her enthusiastic sprints. It does little to muffle the creaking of the floorboards beneath it.

item_hallway_lightswitch.name: light switch
item_hallway_lightswitch.description: A standard toggle light switch, its plastic cover slightly yellowed with age. The hallway’s overhead light flickers ominously when turned on, as if it’s considering whether it’s worth the effort to illuminate your morning chaos. You’ve been meaning to replace the bulb for weeks.

item_hallway_note.name: sticky note
item_hallway_note.description: A neon pink sticky note is affixed to the wall near the light switch, its edges curling slightly. In your own hurried scrawl, it reads: "BUY MILK. Also, don’t forget to feed Pussycat. (She will judge you if you do.)" The ink is smudged in one corner, as if you’d reconsidered the urgency of this reminder at 2 AM.

item_9a7b8c1d.name: toothbrush
item_9a7b8c1d.description: A slightly frayed toothbrush, its bristles splayed from overuse. The mint-green handle is faded, and there’s a suspicious smear of something blue near the base—probably toothpaste, but you’d rather not think about it too hard. It’s yours, though, so you’ll have to make do.

item_e2f3g4h5.name: damp towel
item_e2f3g4h5.description: A once-fluffy blue bath towel, now damp and slightly musty from being left in a heap on the rack. It’s still serviceable, if you’re desperate, but you’d rather not think about the last time it was washed. Pussycat has claimed it as a secondary napping spot on more than one occasion.

item_i6j7k8l9.name: leaky faucet
item_i6j7k8l9.description: A chrome-plated faucet that has seen better days. It drips with infuriating regularity, each *plink* of water into the sink echoing like a countdown to your inevitable lateness. You’ve meant to fix it for weeks, but somehow, it’s always tomorrow’s problem.

item_m1n2o3p4.name: medicine cabinet
item_m1n2o3p4.description: A mirrored medicine cabinet, its surface smudged with fingerprints and the occasional cat paw print. The door is now wide open, revealing a cluttered interior of half-empty bottles, expired prescriptions, and forgotten toiletries. The back of the cabinet is lined with a yellowed newspaper clipping, its edges curled with age.

i_6e0ddf.name: linen closet door
i_6e0ddf.description: A narrow, white-painted door with scuffed edges and slightly creaky hinges. It leads to the linen closet, though calling it a 'closet' might be generous—it’s more of a domestic black hole where towels and random household items go to disappear.

i_b6f540.name: folded towels
i_b6f540.description: A stack of folded towels in various shades of blue and white, some still crisp from the dryer, others slightly rumpled as if they’ve been hastily refolded. They smell faintly of lavender fabric softener, though a few bear the unmistakable imprint of Pussycat’s paws.

i_306060.name: fabric softener bottle
i_306060.description: A half-empty bottle of lavender-scented fabric softener. The label is slightly peeling, and the cap is loose, as if someone (probably you) couldn’t be bothered to screw it back on properly. The liquid inside sloshes ominously when tilted.

i_ffd6fc.name: lumpy laundry pile
i_ffd6fc.description: A precarious heap of laundry, some clean, some questionably so. The pile shifts slightly when disturbed, as if something—or someone—might be buried beneath it. A single sock dangles from the top, swaying ominously. Pussycat’s tail flicks from within, betraying her presence as the architect of this domestic disaster.

item_7a3d8f1e.name: half-empty bag of bread
item_7a3d8f1e.description: A plastic bag of sliced white bread, its twist-tie loosely securing what remains of the loaf. The bread inside is slightly stale, the edges of the slices curling ever so slightly, but it’s still serviceable for a quick breakfast. The label on the bag boasts 'Soft & Fresh!' in cheerful, optimistic lettering, though the reality is a bit more lackluster.

item_9b2e4c7f.name: toaster
item_9b2e4c7f.description: A standard two-slot toaster, its chrome exterior slightly smudged with fingerprints and a few stray crumbs clinging to the lever. The crumb tray at the bottom is half-full, a testament to many hurried breakfasts. A single, forgotten crumb sits in one of the slots, as if waiting for its moment to be reborn as toast. The cord trails off the counter and disappears behind the appliance, plugged into an outlet somewhere out of sight.

item_1c8e5d2a.name: refrigerator
item_1c8e5d2a.description: A white, slightly dented refrigerator that hums with quiet determination. The surface is a mosaic of magnets, coupons, and a few outdated takeout menus, all held in place by the fridge’s weak magnetic field. A child’s drawing of what might be a cat—or possibly a very lopsided house—is stuck to the door with a magnet shaped like a slice of pizza. The handle is cool to the touch, and the faint scent of milk and forgotten leftovers wafts out when you stand close.

item_3f6a1b9c.name: coffee maker
item_3f6a1b9c.description: A black drip coffee maker, its glass carafe stained with the remnants of many mornings. The warming plate beneath it is still slightly warm, a sign that it was used recently, though the carafe itself is empty save for a few stubborn drops of coffee clinging to the bottom. The filter basket holds a crumpled, used paper filter, its edges browned with old grounds. The scent of stale coffee lingers around it, a bittersweet reminder of the day’s first caffeine fix.
